static bool processTrunc(TruncInst *TI, LazyValueInfo *LVI) {
  if (TI->hasNoSignedWrap() && TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap())
    return false;

  ConstantRange Range =
      LVI->getConstantRangeAtUse(TI->getOperandUse(0), /*UndefAllowed=*/false);
  uint64_t DestWidth = TI->getDestTy()->getScalarSizeInBits();
  bool Changed = false;

  if (!TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap()) {
    if (Range.getActiveBits() <= DestWidth) {
      TI->setHasNoUnsignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNUW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }

  if (!TI->hasNoSignedWrap()) {
    if (Range.getMinSignedBits() <= DestWidth) {
      TI->setHasNoSignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNSW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }

  return Changed;
}

static bool runImpl(Function &F, LazyValueInfo *LVI, DominatorTree *DT,
                    const SimplifyQuery &SQ) {
  bool FnChanged = false;

  if (F.hasExactDefinition() && F.getReturnType()->isIntOrIntVectorTy())

  // Visiting in a pre-order depth-first traversal causes us to simplify early
  // blocks before querying later blocks (which require us to analyze early

  for (BasicBlock *BB : depth_first(&F.getEntryBlock())) {

    if (Range.getMinSignedBits() <= DestWidth) {

static bool processTrunc(TruncInst *TI, LazyValueInfo *LVI) {
  if (TI->hasNoSignedWrap() && TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap())
    return false;

  ConstantRange Range =
      LVI->getConstantRangeAtUse(TI->getOperandUse(0), /*UndefAllowed=*/false);
  uint64_t DestWidth = TI->getDestTy()->getScalarSizeInBits();
  bool Changed = false;

  if (!TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap()) {
    if (Range.getActiveBits() <= DestWidth) {
      TI->setHasNoUnsignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNUW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }

  if (!TI->hasNoSignedWrap()) {
    // To have no signed wrap, the truncated value must equal the original
    // when sign-extended back. This requires that all values in Range fit
    // within the signed range of DestWidth.
    APInt SignedMin = APInt::getSignedMinValue(DestWidth).sext(Range.getBitWidth());
    APInt SignedMax = APInt::getSignedMaxValue(DestWidth).sext(Range.getBitWidth());
    ConstantRange SignedRange(SignedMin, SignedMax + 1);
    if (SignedRange.contains(Range)) {
      TI->setHasNoSignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNSW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }
  }

  return Changed;
}

static bool processTrunc(TruncInst *TI, LazyValueInfo *LVI) {
  if (TI->hasNoSignedWrap() && TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap())
    return false;

  ConstantRange Range = LVI->getConstantRange(TI->getOperand(0), TI->getParent(), /*UndefAllowed=*/false);
  uint64_t DestWidth = TI->getDestTy()->getScalarSizeInBits();
  bool Changed = false;

  if (!TI->hasNoUnsignedWrap()) {
    if (Range.getActiveBits() <= DestWidth) {
      TI->setHasNoUnsignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNUW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }

  if (!TI->hasNoSignedWrap()) {
    // To have no signed wrap, the truncated value must equal the original
    // when sign-extended back. This requires that all values in Range fit
    // within the signed range of DestWidth.
    APInt SignedMin = APInt::getSignedMinValue(DestWidth).sext(Range.getBitWidth());
    APInt SignedMax = APInt::getSignedMaxValue(DestWidth).sext(Range.getBitWidth());
    ConstantRange SignedRange(SignedMin, SignedMax + 1);
    if (SignedRange.contains(Range)) {
      TI->setHasNoSignedWrap(true);
      ++NumNSW;
      Changed = true;
    }
  }

  return Changed;
}
</output>
